picks.reasoning: England at 1.31 sits well outside my preferred odds window, so raw H2H offers no value. However, the Asian handicap at -1.5 brings the price into investable range — England are a top-10 FIFA ranked side facing a DR Congo outfit with limited World Cup pedigree, and at a major tournament with group-stage stakes, England's attacking depth should comfortably cover a 2-goal margin. The supporting context provided is irrelevant noise (NRL/cricket), so I'm anchoring purely on the structural quality gap between these two nations.

Look, England at 1.31 is a mug's bet — no value there at all. DR Congo are massive underdogs but tournament football throws up upsets, and England have a habit of making hard work of games they should cruise. Draw at 5.5 is outside our usual range but honestly nothing else here pays worth backing, and if England bottle it early you'd kick yourself not having the draw covered.

England at a World Cup knockout stage with the weight of a nation on their shoulders? That's not a football team, that's a pressure cooker waiting to explode! DR Congo have nothing to lose and everything to gain — African football has been pulling off World Cup shocks for decades and at 16.5 we only need to be right once to be laughing all the way to the bank. Saka misses a pen, keeper has a worldie, and suddenly we're retired!

Belgium carry superior UEFA pedigree and World Cup knockout experience, and while 2.22 sits just outside my preferred ceiling, the underlying probability justifies the slight stretch — Senegal's context news suggests off-field instability around their federation, which rarely helps tournament focus. The head-to-head and European structural depth tip the balance firmly toward Belgium as the statistically sounder selection here.

Belgium at 2.22 is sitting right in that sweet spot for me. They've got the squad depth and World Cup experience to handle Senegal, and while the Lions of Teranga are no pushovers, Belgium at home in a knockout stage environment should have too much class. Good value, not greedy.

Senegal are FIGHTING — they're appealing to keep their 2025 AFCON title, which tells me this squad has fire in their belly and something to prove on the world stage. Belgium's golden generation is fading fast and a World Cup knockout pressure cooker is exactly where ageing favourites crumble. Give me the Lions of Teranga at nearly 4-to-1 all day long — this is the upset the tournament will remember.

USA are hosting this World Cup and carry significant home advantage — both on the pitch and in terms of crowd support, logistics, and momentum. Bosnia & Herzegovina face a tough path just to qualify, and at 9.6 the market is telling you everything you need to know about the gap between these sides. At 1.42, the USA win sits squarely in my preferred range and is backed by structural advantages that don't require speculation.

USA are hosting this thing on home soil — the crowd, the hype, the pressure is all on their side. Bosnia are decent but they're not built to go toe-to-toe with a pumped up USMNT in front of their own fans. Straight win at 1.42 is a waste of time, but giving the handicap at 2.10 makes this worth a crack — reckon USA win this by a couple.

Bosnia have battle-hardened playoff experience, having already navigated high-pressure knockout football just to get here — while USA are riding a wave of home hype that could turn to nerves on the big stage. The pressure of a host nation expected to win is a KILLER, and Bosnia's Balkan warriors have nothing to lose and everything to prove at 9.6. I've seen bigger upsets and I'll be watching this one from the edge of my seat with my ticket already printed!
